prose:
Fukui reactivity indices computed for acrolein (CH₂=CH–CHO, 8 atoms) at DFT B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier 'standard') in gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1, using PySCF via the chemkit fukui-reactivity skill. Charges from Mulliken population analysis on the neutral, cation (N−1, charge +1, doublet), and anion (N+1, charge −1, doublet) species. Geometry used as-is from the supplied .xyz file; no re-optimization was performed. All integrity checks passed (trustworthy: true); sum(f+) = 1.0000, sum(f−) = 1.0000.

Most electrophilic site: Atom 4 (C, β-carbon), f⁺ = 0.64232102 — consistent with Michael acceptor reactivity.
Most nucleophilic site: Atom 1 (O, carbonyl oxygen), f⁻ = 0.39388616.

Fukui indices by atom (1-based): f⁺ = [0.40136419, 0.022255106, 0.43404673, 0.64232102, −0.1572595, −0.2072029, −0.062116996, −0.073407645]; f⁻ = [0.39388616, 0.0059884353, 0.091403097, 0.087369815, 0.085961423, 0.1856796, 0.084485809, 0.065225658]; dual descriptor Δf = [0.0074780351, 0.01626667, 0.34264363, 0.5549512, −0.24322092, −0.39288251, −0.14660281, −0.1386333].
